Thought 4: The narrative provides a critical detail: "Addison was present in the fitness center at the time of the murder." This directly places Addison at the scene when the crime occurred. For Irving, while he stayed later than Uma, the narrative does not confirm he was present at the exact time of the murder. Additionally, the vial Irving carried and his expertise with corrosive substances are circumstantial, but Addison has both motive (Uma could expose his theft) and opportunity (he was there at the time). The custom-made suit suggests unexplained income, possibly from stolen equipment, reinforcing his motive. Therefore, Addison is the most likely murderer.
